original_hypothesis.title: Tokenomics.checkpoint() Lacks Reentrancy Guard Despite External Call to Treasury Before Epoch Increment
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The checkpoint() function makes an external call to ITreasury(treasury).rebalanceTreasury(incentives[1]) BEFORE incrementing epochCounter. The _locked reentrancy variable exists in the contract but is NOT checked in checkpoint(). If the treasury contract is compromised or has a callback path, it could re-enter checkpoint() and process the same epoch data twice, causing double inflation and double reward allocation.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The state changes for the current epoch (endTime, totalTopUpsOLAS, effectiveBond, maxBond, etc.) are all written BEFORE the treasury call, but epochCounter is incremented AFTER. A re-entrant call would see the same epochCounter and attempt to process the same epoch, leading to state corruption.

proof_of_signal.pattern: ITreasury(treasury).rebalanceTreasury(incentives[1]) called at line 697 before epochCounter increment at line 701; no _locked check in function

preconditions: Treasury contract has a callback mechanism that can call checkpoint()
Treasury contract is compromised or intentionally malicious
The re-entrant checkpoint call passes timing checks

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: External call to treasury before epochCounter increment; _locked variable exists but not used in checkpoint()
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Re-entrant checkpoint would use same prevEpochTime and eCounter, potentially double-processing inflation and rewards
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: _locked reentrancy guard not checked in checkpoint() function

analysis_questions: What are the exact state changes before vs after the treasury call?
Would a re-entrant checkpoint pass the diffNumSeconds check?
Could the treasury contract be upgraded to introduce a callback?
validation_requirements: Create a mock treasury that re-enters checkpoint during rebalanceTreasury
Verify if the re-entrant call processes the epoch again
Check for double-counting of inflation, effectiveBond, and staking incentives

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised treasury re-enters checkpoint during rebalanceTreasury. The second checkpoint call sees unchanged epochCounter and processes the same epoch data, doubling inflation allocation and effectiveBond credits. epochCounter could increment twice.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Double inflation, double effectiveBond, double staking incentives for one epoch — protocol-breaking
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires protocol pause and manual state correction

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No reentrancy test for checkpoint via treasury callback

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by epochCounter jumping by 2 in one block

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
